evidence-binding: fix-overlap-fail-closed d4ec59d99991
<!-- RED: paste RED here -->
RED: Before the fix, flipped the false-GREEN assertions to fail-closed expectations and confirmed all three fail against the unmodified (fail-open, #660) classifiers:
- `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` — `testClassifierFastScopePreSectionUnclosedFenceRed` threw: `Error: a claimed fast project with a structurally-ambiguous ## Scope (unclosed fence) must fail closed to red; expected red, got green` (scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:5465).
- `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js` — the `fast-fence-pre` case threw `AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: actual: 'green', expected: 'red'` at test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js:954.
- `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js` — the `fast-fence-pre` case threw `AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: actual: 'green', expected: 'red'` at test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js:885.
All three confirm the pre-fix consumer (`scanClaimedOverlap` reading `sectionBody`) silently collapses an ambiguous claimed ## Scope into "no write set" → false GREEN on a genuinely overlapping candidate.
<!-- GREEN: paste GREEN here -->
GREEN: Fixed at the CONSUMER ONLY (scanClaimedOverlap in all 4 classifier editions): the claimed fast-summary Scope is now read via the already-exported `sectionBodyState(...)` (no new export) instead of `sectionBody(...)`. When `status === 'ambiguous'` (unclosed fence or duplicate `## Scope` heading), the claimed project's write set is treated as INDETERMINATE and folded into a new `hasAmbiguousScope`/`ambiguousScopeProject` result field; `classify()` checks it first and returns `verdict:'red'` with reasoning `'claimed project "<project>" fast-summary.md has a structurally ambiguous/unparseable ## Scope section (unclosed fence or duplicate heading); write set indeterminate; conservative red'`. `status === 'absent'` (no heading at all) is UNCHANGED — no overlap is manufactured. The primitives `sectionBody`/`sectionBodyState` are byte-unchanged (still return `''`/`{status:'ambiguous'}`).
Tests: after the fix, all three flipped assertions pass GREEN:
- `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` → exits 0, "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed"; `testClassifierFastScopePreSectionUnclosedFenceRed: PASSED` and the new companion `testClassifierFastScopeAbsentNotManufacturedOverlap: PASSED` (absent Scope still green, not manufactured into an overlap).
- `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js` → all assertions pass (no ERR_ASSERTION), including the flipped `fast-fence-pre` case now `red`.
- `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js` → all assertions pass (no ERR_ASSERTION), including the flipped `fast-fence-pre` case now `red`.
`node scripts/validate-script-sync.js` and `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` both clean (byte-identical canonical pair + edition-sync unaffected).
